column1: # Historical group-stage disciplinary records for the FIFA fair-play tiebreaker.
# Scope: World Cups that USED the fair-play points tiebreaker (introduced 2018) -> 2018
# One row = one team's GROUP STAGE (3 matches) in one tournament.
# Card columns are raw counts; build_fair_play.py converts them to FIFA points:
# A team's prior = average of its per-tournament group-stage fair-play points.
# Teams with NO row here inherit the all-team average (see build_fair_play.py).
# DATA CONFIDENCE: only the two rows below are independently verified (the 2018
# Japan/Senegal case that the tiebreaker famously decided: Japan 4 yellows = -4
# Senegal 6 yellows = -6). Remaining 2018/2022 group-stage card counts still need
# a verified pass (Wikipedia per-match reports / FIFA technical reports). Add rows
# here and re-run build_fair_play.py; no engine changes needed.
